title: clinopodium axillare subsp. uniflorum
intro: Clinopodium axillare subsp. uniflorum, also known as Brazilian marjoram or Mexican oregano, is a plant species belonging to the mint family (Lamiaceae). Traditionally, it has been used for its medicinal properties, including its antiseptic, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which are attributed to its essential oils and flavonoids. The plant is believed to offer various health benefits, such as relieving digestive issues, reducing inflammation, and aiding in the treatment of respiratory problems, fever, and skin conditions. Clinopodium axillare subsp. uniflorum can be prepared as teas, infusions, or essential oils, and is also used in traditional herbal medicine to support overall well-being and immune function.
